paper_title: Quaternary Landforms and Deposits Study in Modrejce Valley

methods.description: This method involves the study of Quaternary landforms and deposits using geomorphological and geological mapping techniques to create detailed maps and cross-sections of the area.

steps.description: A map of Quaternary deposits in the wider Tolmin area was summarized from Šifrer (1965), Buser (1987) and our GIS-and field-based geomorphological and geological mapping.
steps.searchable_summary: summarize Quaternary deposits map from existing studies and GIS data

steps.description: A detailed map of Quaternary landforms in the selected area of Modrejce was prepared by means of field-based geomorphological and geological mapping.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare detailed map of Quaternary landforms using field-based mapping

steps.description: Geomorphological mapping was based on Li-DAR data with a relative horizontal and vertical accuracy of 0.30 and 0.15 m, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: use Li-DAR data for geomorphological mapping

steps.description: The Li-DAR-based DEM with 1 m resolution was used to generate a shaded relief map, a slope degree map, a slope aspect map and contour lines with an equidistance of 1 m.
steps.searchable_summary: generate topographic maps and contour lines from Li-DAR DEM

methods.description: This method documents Quaternary deposits using field sedimentological logging and classifies them based on lithology and sedimentary characteristics to interpret depositional environments.

steps.description: Quaternary deposits were documented using field sedimentological logging.
steps.searchable_summary: document Quaternary deposits through field sedimentological logging

steps.description: Each unit was described according to its lithology using classifications by Wenthworth (1922) and Folk et al. (1970) and according to its various sedimentary characteristics described by Tucker (2011).
steps.searchable_summary: classify lithology and sedimentary characteristics of units

steps.description: Facies are summarized according to Eyles et al. (1983) and Miall (2006) codes respectively for glacigenic and alluvial/deltaic deposits.
steps.searchable_summary: summarize facies using Eyles et al. and Miall codes

steps.description: Facies associations are summarized as in Miall (2006) and Benn and Evans (2010).
steps.searchable_summary: summarize facies associations using Miall and Benn & Evans
